title: Around the House: Rooms, Objects, and Location
objectives: Name rooms and household objects and describe where things are.
Understand Carlos in short guided exchanges.
Use the lesson language in one realistic conversation.
objective: Name rooms and household objects and describe where things are.

grammar.explanation: Use estar with location phrases: est? en, est? al lado de, est? debajo de, est? encima de.

examples.spanish: La llave est? en la mesa.
examples.english: The key is on the table.

examples.spanish: El tel?fono est? al lado de la cama.
examples.english: The phone is next to the bed.

grammar.practicePrompt: Say one original sentence using today?s pattern with Carlos.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.spanish: Vamos a practicar around the house.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.spanish: Muy bien. Usa una frase completa.
lines.english: Very good. Use a complete sentence.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: Excuse me, I have a question.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: How do you say it with your family?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.spanish: Lo digo despacio y con respeto.
lines.english: I say it slowly and respectfully.

culture.title: Around the House in Real Life
culture.text: In many Spanish-speaking homes, the kitchen and dining table are important social spaces. Visitors may be invited to sit and talk there.

cultureNotes: In many Spanish-speaking homes, the kitchen and dining table are important social spaces. Visitors may be invited to sit and talk there.

nativeSpeech.title: How Carlos would say it naturally
notes: ?Mi casa es tu casa? is a warm hospitality phrase, though often said playfully.

commonMistakes.correction: La llave est? en la mesa.
commonMistakes.explanation: Use estar for where things are.

commonMistakes.correction: Habitaci?n can mean bedroom or room depending on context.

realLifeMission.title: Real Life Mission
realLifeMission.mission: Give Carlos a mini tour of your home using three rooms and two location phrases.
successCriteria: Use at least three lesson vocabulary items.
Answer one follow-up question from Carlos.

review.summary: You practiced around the house for everyday Spanish.
canDo: Answer Carlos with simple complete sentences.
Use the Real Life Mission in a practical situation.
